description:
Chain rename — three connected containers all renamed together (api → apiV2, dbRepo → primaryDbRepo, db → primaryDb). Realistic label evolution preserves enough technology/tags anchors that the leaf (db ↔ primaryDb, same tech PostgreSQL, both empty relations) crosses threshold first; iteration 2 catches the middle (primaryDbRepo ↔ dbRepo, now with confirmed db→primaryDb in renameMap so relation jaccard = 1); iteration 3 catches the head. Without fixed-point iteration, raw relation jaccard sees all-renamed targets and falls to 0, killing the chain.

Confidence on the chain leaf (primaryDb) lands in the LOW band — it crosses threshold mostly on technology + empty-relations match, no transitive bonus available. Each subsequent chain element gets HIGH confidence because the relation jaccard transitively confirms via the leaf's rename.
